data.body:
**Completion verification ‚Äî PASS**

Checked each acceptance criterion against the repository:

**AC1 ‚Äî ProductionBatchRunner adapts sizing.py to the BatchRunner seam, journaling an opaque batch-plan.** MET. `src/rebar/llm/plan_review/production_batch_runner.py` implements `ProductionBatchRunner(BatchRunner)` importing from `rebar.llm.workflow.runners` (`BatchRunner`, `BatchRunRequest`, `BatchRunResult`). It delegates all sizing/ladder/budget/checkpoint/shed logic to `pass1.run_pass1` (which internally calls `sizing.pass1_with_ladder`, `sizing.shed_to_budget`, `sizing.plan_budget_cap`, `sizing.load_checkpoint`, `sizing.save_checkpoint`). The `coverage` dict returned as `batch_plan` in `BatchRunResult.outputs` is the opaque plan the interpreter stores but never branches on.

**AC2 ‚Äî Unit tests for equivalence (packing, escalation, shed-to-budget, checkpoint resume) + seam conformance.** MET. `tests/unit/workflow/test_production_batch_runner.py` contains real (non-skipped, non-xfailed) tests covering: `test_runner_findings_equal_bespoke_run_pass1` (equivalence), `test_size_ladder_escalation_through_runner_equals_bespoke` (batch‚Üí1-crit‚Üímodel-ladder‚Üítoo-big, with `_BatchContextLimitRunner` and `_AllContextLimitRunner`), `test_shed_to_budget_ordering_through_runner_equals_bespoke` (overlay before core ordering), `test_checkpoint_resume_through_runner_equals_bespoke` (resume by material fingerprint), and `test_seam_conformance_and_agent_runner_unused` (opaque plan + no-branch contract).

**AC3 ‚Äî sizing.py remains the single home of sizing logic; new module is a thin adapter.** MET. `production_batch_runner.py` contains no sizing/ladder/budget/shed algorithm. All computation flows through `run_pass1` ‚Üí `sizing.*`. The adapter is ~186 LOC with no duplicated algorithm.

**AC4 ‚Äî `make lint` + full test suite pass.** Not directly verifiable from read-only tools. The tests have no skip/xfail markers and the code imports cleanly; structurally consistent with passing. Runtime CI result cannot be confirmed from the repository state alone.

Overall verdict: **PASS** ‚Äî all three verifiable acceptance criteria are clearly met by the implementation; AC4 is a CI/runtime gate outside the scope of static code verification.
